basic_info.title: Bravely Default
basic_info.review: Bravely Default is a masterpiece within the RPG genre that combines nostalgia and modernity with its innovative Brave/Default battle system, deep job customization, and compelling parallel world narrative.

alternative_names: Bravely Default: Flying Fairy (クロノ・トリガー)
Bravely Default: Where the Fairy Flies

similar_games.title: Final Fantasy: The 4 Heroes of Light

play_today.details: The definitive HD Remaster version with 60FPS performance, widescreen aspect ratio, redesigned UI, and comprehensive quality of life improvements. This is the best way to experience Bravely Default in 2025-2026.

description.synopsis: Four heroes travel through parallel worlds to awaken four elemental crystals consumed by darkness, uncovering a conspiracy involving a mysterious fairy and an ancient evil known as Ouroboros.
key_features: Innovative Brave/Default battle system with risk/reward mechanics
Deep job class system with 24 unique jobs and ability combination
Parallel world narrative with multiple endings based on player choices
Bravely Second ability to freeze time during battles (For the Sequel)
Auto-battle and fast-forward options for quality of life
description.long_description: Bravely Default, known in Japan as Bravely Default: Flying Fairy, is a 2012 role-playing video game developed by Silicon Studio and published by Square Enix for the Nintendo 3DS. Originally conceived as a sequel to Final Fantasy: The 4 Heroes of Light, it evolved into its own intellectual property while retaining classic JRPG elements. The game features an innovative turn-based battle system built around Brave Points (BP) that allow players to either bank actions for later use or borrow from future turns at the cost of waiting. The job system offers deep customization with 24 unique jobs that can be combined and leveled independently. The story follows four protagonists—Agnès Oblige, Tiz Arrior, Ringabel, and Edea Lee—as they traverse parallel worlds to awaken elemental crystals consumed by darkness. The game was composed by Revo of Sound Horizon, character designs handled by Akihiko Yoshida (Final Fantasy Tactics), and received critical acclaim for its gameplay mechanics, story, and presentation. An expanded edition titled For the Sequel added quality of life improvements including auto-battle, difficulty settings, and encounter rate adjustments. The HD Remaster released in 2025-2026 further enhanced the experience with 60FPS performance, widescreen support, redesigned UI, and additional quality of life features.
legacy_and_impact: Revitalized interest in traditional turn-based JRPGs during the handheld era
Popularized the Brave/Default battle mechanic that influenced later Square Enix titles
Demonstrated commercial viability for Japanese-style RPGs in Western markets
Led to multiple sequels including Bravely Second: End Layer and Bravely Default II
Inspired Octopath Traveler through shared development team members
Maintained strong community engagement years after release with active modding scene
